description:
You are a forensic examiner investigating suspected intellectual property theft. Employee Jordan Hayes is accused of exfiltrating proprietary documents to a personal USB drive before departing the company. You have:

1. /home/ga/evidence/corporate_repo.dd — NTFS snapshot of the corporate shared file repository
2. /home/ga/evidence/suspect_usb.dd — NTFS image of the suspect's seized USB drive
3. /home/ga/evidence/corporate_hashes.txt — MD5 hash inventory of all corporate files (one 'md5  filename' per line)

Create an Autopsy case named 'IP_Theft_2024' with case number 'INV-IPT-0892'. Import corporate_hashes.txt as a Notable hash set named 'Corporate_IP' in Autopsy. Add both disk images as separate data sources and run ingest with Hash Lookup (using the Corporate_IP set) and File Type Identification modules.

Produce /home/ga/Reports/exfiltration_report.txt with these pipe-delimited sections:

## EXFILTRATED_FILES — USB files matching corporate hashes. Columns: USB_FILENAME|USB_PATH|MD5|FILE_SIZE

## MODIFIED_COPIES — USB files with same name as a corporate file but different MD5. Columns: FILENAME|USB_MD5|CORPORATE_MD5|USB_SIZE

## DELETED_USB_FILES — All deleted files recovered from the USB. Columns: FILENAME|SIZE|MD5

## CONCEALED_DATA — NTFS Alternate Data Streams on the USB. Columns: HOST_FILE|STREAM_NAME|STREAM_SIZE

## INVESTIGATION_SUMMARY — Total counts per category, most-targeted department, and risk classification (LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH/CRITICAL).
